content: If we treat the human language learning definition of reflexion (as a definable, teachable vocabulary term for deliberate reflection in high school literacy) as a blueprint, we can construct more grounded, interpretable LLM self-reflection mechanisms by anchoring abstract self-correction to explicit, codified reflection templates like those used to teach critical reflection to students. This turns the informal, variable self-reflection of current LLMs into a learnable skill structured the same way we teach human reflection to novice learners.
